landing.hero_headline: Know your AI compliance gaps before regulators do.
landing.hero_subheadline: GapSight is a free self-assessment tool for ML teams. Enter your evaluation metrics, get a structured gap report relative to EU AI Act, NIST AI RMF, and ISO/IEC 42001.
landing.hero_disclaimer: Informative self-assessment tool. Not legal advice. Informational only.

how_steps.title: Get your gap report
how_steps.description: CRITICAL / HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW risk level with action items.

how_steps.title: Export or share
how_steps.description: HTML and JSON export, shareable link.

frameworks.name: EU AI Act
frameworks.description: The European regulation establishing harmonized rules for artificial intelligence systems based on risk classification.

frameworks.name: NIST AI RMF
frameworks.description: The U.S. framework for managing risks throughout the AI system lifecycle across govern, map, measure, and manage functions.

frameworks.name: ISO/IEC 42001
frameworks.description: The international standard specifying requirements for establishing and maintaining an AI management system.

meta.title: GapSight — AI Regulatory Self-Assessment for ML Teams
meta.description: GapSight is an informative self-assessment tool that helps AI teams identify potential gaps relative to EU AI Act, NIST AI RMF, and ISO/IEC 42001.

disclaimer_bar.text: GapSight — AI regulatory self-assessment for ML teams. Results are not legal advice.

footer.tagline: GapSight — AI regulatory self-assessment for ML teams.

about.title: GapSight
about.subtitle: AI regulatory self-assessment for ML teams.
about.what_body: GapSight is an informative self-assessment tool that helps AI teams identify potential gaps in their evaluation practices relative to EU AI Act, NIST AI RMF, and ISO/IEC 42001 requirements.
what_not_items: Not a platform for regulatory compliance
Not a substitute for qualified legal counsel
about.who_body: ML engineers and AI product teams who want a structured starting point for understanding which regulatory requirements may apply to their system, based on their own evaluation metrics.
about.how_body: As a checklist to identify areas that may need attention, not as a certificate of regulatory compliance. Always consult a qualified professional before making deployment decisions.

tos.title: Terms of Service
tos.definition: GapSight is an informative self-assessment tool. It does not provide legal advice and is not a certification system.
tos.liability: Liability is limited to the accuracy of the knowledge base at the time of assessment generation.
tos.user_responsibility: You are solely responsible for all decisions based on the results provided by GapSight. Always consult a qualified professional before making deployment decisions.
tos.kb_currency: GapSight does not guarantee real-time accuracy of regulatory information. The knowledge base version is always visible in the interface and in all exports.
tos.language: GapSight is available in English only.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.data_collected_body: GapSight stores session data in your browser's localStorage. Server-side storage occurs only when you explicitly create a share link, using Vercel KV with a 12-month TTL. No personally identifiable information is collected.
privacy.third_parties_body: GapSight does not share your data with any third parties.
privacy.user_rights_body: You may delete any share link at any time. For GDPR-related inquiries, contact us at the email below.
privacy.role_body: GapSight acts as data processor. You, the user, are the data controller.

kb_changelog.title: Knowledge Base Changelog
kb_changelog.description: History of changes to the GapSight knowledge base, including framework references, thresholds, and metrics.

threshold_label: GapSight default — not a regulatory requirement

inline_disclaimer.title: Before you view results
inline_disclaimer.body: GapSight is an informative self-assessment tool. Results are based on publicly available regulatory frameworks and the metrics you provide. This is not legal advice and not a certificate of regulatory compliance. Always consult a qualified professional.

clear_session_modal.title: Clear Session
clear_session_modal.body: This will delete all assessment data stored in your browser. This action cannot be undone.

exports.disclaimer: This report is an informative self-assessment generated by GapSight. It is not legal advice and not a certificate of regulatory compliance. Always consult a qualified professional before making deployment decisions.
exports.canonical_note: Generated by GapSight — AI regulatory self-assessment for ML teams. Based on publicly available regulatory frameworks.

feedback.title: Report an Issue
feedback.success_message: Thank you. Your feedback has been received.
feedback.error_message: Something went wrong. Please try again or contact us at feedback@gapsight.dev.
feedback.timeout_message: The request timed out. Please try again or contact us directly at feedback@gapsight.dev.

errors.generic: Something went wrong. Please try again.
errors.network: Network error. Please check your connection and try again.

assessment.continuing_previous: Continuing previous assessment. Your inputs have been restored.

deployment.title: Deployment Context
deployment.question: Is your AI system already in production?

frameworks.title: Framework Selection
frameworks.subtitle: Answer a few questions to determine which regulatory frameworks apply to your system.
frameworks.q1: Do users of your system currently reside in the EU, or is your company registered in the EU?
frameworks.q2: Is your company based in the US, or do you work with US federal agencies or US-regulated clients?
frameworks.q3: Do your clients or partners require ISO certification, or is it a contractual requirement in your industry?
frameworks.q4: Are you looking for a globally recognized framework for internal AI risk governance, without certification requirements?
frameworks.q5: Do you plan to expand to the EU market within the next 12 months?
frameworks.reason_eu_mandatory: The EU AI Act applies to your system as a legal obligation.
frameworks.reason_eu_proactive: Proactive preparation for EU market expansion.
frameworks.reason_nist_us: NIST AI RMF is recommended for US-based or US-regulated operations.
frameworks.reason_nist_global: NIST AI RMF serves as a de facto global standard for AI risk governance.
frameworks.reason_nist_default: NIST AI RMF is recommended as a starting point for AI risk governance.
frameworks.reason_iso: ISO/IEC 42001 is flagged based on your certification or contractual requirements.
frameworks.eu_extraterritorial_note: The EU AI Act applies extraterritorially — if your users are in the EU, the regulation applies regardless of where your company is based.
frameworks.override_warning: You are manually adjusting the suggested frameworks. GapSight will assess against all selected frameworks.
frameworks.additional_label: You can also include these frameworks:

onboarding.title: Onboarding

role_options.provider: Provider — builds and places AI system on market
role_options.deployer: Deployer — uses third-party AI system in own business

onboarding.gpai_question: Is your system a foundation model, large language model, or other general-purpose AI model that you place on the market for downstream use by third parties?
onboarding.risk_subtitle: Answer the following questions to determine your system's risk category.
onboarding.risk_p1: Does your system make or directly influence decisions that affect natural persons?
onboarding.risk_p2: Is the system used in: employment, education, healthcare, justice, critical infrastructure, migration, biometrics, or access to essential services?
onboarding.risk_p3: Does the system generate content or interact with users in a way that could mislead them about the nature of the interaction?
onboarding.risk_p4: Is the system a safety component of a product regulated by EU product safety law (medical devices, vehicles, aviation, etc.)?
onboarding.risk_override_warning: You are overriding the suggested classification. Responsibility for incorrect classification rests with you.

assessment.section_subtitle: This section helps identify potential gaps relative to {framework} requirements. Thresholds shown are GapSight industry defaults, not regulatory requirements.

accuracy_performance.title: Section 1: Accuracy & Performance
accuracy_performance.framework: EU AI Act Article 15, NIST AI RMF, ISO 42001

fairness_bias.title: Section 2: Fairness & Bias
fairness_bias.framework: EU AI Act Article 10, NIST AI RMF, ISO 42001
fairness_bias.blocker_message: EU AI Act Article 10 requires bias detection for high-risk systems. This section is mandatory for your profile.
fairness_bias.no_metrics_body: Use Fairlearn or AIF360 to calculate fairness metrics for your model.
fairness_bias.no_metrics_button: I have not calculated these metrics

robustness_drift.title: Section 3: Robustness, Drift & Cybersecurity
robustness_drift.framework: EU AI Act Article 15, NIST AI RMF, ISO 42001

explainability_transparency.title: Section 4: Explainability & Transparency
explainability_transparency.framework: EU AI Act Article 13, NIST AI RMF, ISO 42001
explainability_transparency.model_card_help: Consider using Google Model Cards or HuggingFace Model Card templates.
explainability_transparency.instructions_critical: Instructions for use documentation is required before deployment for high-risk systems.

human_oversight.title: Section 5: Human Oversight
human_oversight.framework: EU AI Act Article 14, NIST AI RMF, ISO 42001
human_oversight.q1: Can operators understand outputs and limitations?
human_oversight.q2: Is there an override or stop mechanism?
human_oversight.q5: Does system actively prevent automation bias?

gpai.title: Section 6: GPAI Obligations
gpai.systemic_risk_warning: Your model exceeds the GPAI systemic risk threshold (10^25 FLOPs). Additional obligations apply from 2 Aug 2025: model evaluations, adversarial testing, incident reporting, cybersecurity measures, notification to EU AI Office.

governance.title: Section 7: Governance
governance.framework: EU AI Act Articles 9-17, NIST AI RMF, ISO 42001
governance.annex_iv_subtitle: Check each element that is documented. Unchecked items will generate action items.
governance.evidence_placeholder: Describe the evidence or documentation available

fields.test_set_representative: Is the test set representative of production data?
fields.drift_monitoring_active: Is drift monitoring active in production?
fields.explanations_available_to_users: Are explanations available to end users?
fields.training_flops_tooltip: FLOPs (Floating Point Operations) measure the total compute used to train your model. Find this value in your training logs, model card, or cloud provider billing dashboard. The EU AI Act systemic risk threshold is 1e25 FLOPs (Article 51). If you trained on a cluster, sum FLOPs across all nodes and the full training run.
fields.systemic_risk_notification_sent: Systemic risk notification sent to EU AI Office?

validation.small_test_set: Metrics may not be reliable with small test sets.
validation.very_small_test_set: Test set below 30 samples — all accuracy metrics will be marked REVIEW regardless of value.

results.title: Assessment Results
results.subtitle: Based on your inputs and the GapSight knowledge base.

share.title: Share Assessment
share.pin_description: Recipients will need this PIN to view the assessment. Share it separately.
share.no_pin_warning: Anyone with the link will be able to view this assessment without a PIN.
share.create_error: Failed to create share link. Please try again.
share.pin_reminder: Remember to share the PIN separately with your recipient.

shared_view.title: Shared Assessment
shared_view.not_found: This shared assessment was not found or has expired.
shared_view.kb_version_warning: This assessment was generated with KB v{version}. Thresholds and mappings may have changed. Consider re-running your assessment.
shared_view.disclaimer: Informative self-assessment only. Not legal advice. Not a certificate of regulatory compliance.
